metadata.quranic_reference: سورة النور ٢٨: بِإِذْنِ أَهْلِهِ - 'by the permission of their owners' (Al-Nur 28)

nda_digital_standard.enforceability: Legally binding in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain

recitals.recital_1: WHEREAS, the Disclosing Party possesses proprietary and confidential information related to [SPECIFY: Technology, Business Plans, Trade Secrets, Customer Lists, Financial Data, AI Models, Source Code, etc.]
recitals.recital_2: WHEREAS, the parties wish to enter into business discussions and evaluate a potential partnership regarding [SPECIFY: Joint venture, API integration, technology licensing, distribution rights, etc.]
recitals.recital_3: WHEREAS, in connection with such discussions, the Disclosing Party may disclose to the Receiving Party certain confidential and proprietary information
recitals.recital_4: WHEREAS, the parties desire to protect such confidential information and establish clear terms governing its use, disclosure, and protection

def_1.definition: All information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party, whether in written, oral, electronic, or visual form, including but not limited to: business plans, financial information, technical data, source code, algorithms, AI models, customer lists, trade secrets, strategic information, and any information marked 'Confidential' or that would reasonably be understood as confidential given its nature and circumstances of disclosure

def_2.definition: Electronically maintained copies of Confidential Information including: encrypted databases, cloud storage, digital documents, blockchain hashes for immutable records, and any digitally-signed versions

def_3.definition: Employees, contractors, and advisors of Receiving Party who have a legitimate 'need-to-know' for the specific business purpose and who are bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as stringent as this Agreement

def_4.definition: Use of Confidential Information solely for the stated business purpose: [SPECIFY: Evaluating partnership, developing APIs, integrating systems, etc.] and NOT for competitive purposes or any other unauthorized use

obligation_1.title: Protection of Confidential Information
obligation_1.description: Receiving Party shall protect Confidential Information with the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care
technical_measures: Encryption of all digital records (AES-256 minimum)
Secure deletion protocols (DOD 5220.22-M standard or better)
Regular security audits and penetration testing

obligation_2.title: Limited Disclosure
obligation_2.description: Receiving Party shall disclose Confidential Information only to Authorized Personnel who have a legitimate need-to-know and who are bound by written confidentiality obligations
obligation_2.enforcement: Receiving Party shall maintain a register of all Authorized Personnel with access to Confidential Information and provide audit reports quarterly

obligation_3.title: Permitted Use Only
obligation_3.description: Confidential Information shall be used solely for the stated Permitted Use. Any other use is prohibited.
prohibited_uses: Reverse engineering or decompilation (especially for AI models, source code)
Disclosure to third parties without written consent
Commercial use beyond the Permitted Use
Sharing with government bodies unless legally compelled

obligation_4.title: Digital Watermarking & Tracking
obligation_4.description: For sensitive AI models, source code, and trade secrets: Receiving Party shall implement digital watermarking and access logging to track if Confidential Information is accessed, by whom, when, and for how long
obligation_4.technology: Blockchain-verifiable audit trails, cryptographic signatures, timestamp-certified logs

obligation_5.title: Data Privacy & Islamic Principles
obligation_5.description: Receiving Party shall comply with all applicable data protection laws and Islamic principles of trust (Amanah) and secrecy (Sirr)
requirements: No use of Confidential Information for surveillance or unethical purposes
Respect user privacy (if Confidential Information includes personal data)
Comply with Saudi DPA, GDPR (if applicable), and Sharia principles
No profiling or manipulation of data for commercial advantage at users' expense

exception_1.title: Public Domain Information
exception_1.description: Information that is, or becomes, publicly available through no breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party is not considered Confidential Information. Burden of proof is on Receiving Party.
exception_1.caveat: Public domain status must be documented and submitted to Disclosing Party for verification

exception_2.title: Legally Required Disclosure
exception_2.description: If Receiving Party is legally compelled to disclose Confidential Information by court order, regulatory demand, or legal obligation, Receiving Party shall: (1) Immediately notify Disclosing Party, (2) Cooperate to seek protective order, (3) Disclose only the minimum legally required, (4) Request confidential treatment from the legal authority
exception_2.process: Minimum 10 business days' notice required before any disclosure

exception_3.title: Prior Knowledge
exception_3.description: Information that Receiving Party can prove it possessed prior to disclosure with independent evidence is not subject to confidentiality obligations
exception_3.proof_required: Original documents, dated communications, or credible witnesses demonstrating prior possession

agreement_duration.renewal: May be renewed for additional 3-year periods by mutual written consent
agreement_duration.termination_by_either_party: Either party may terminate with 30 days' written notice

confidentiality_survive.duration_after_termination: Confidentiality obligations survive termination for an additional 5 years
confidentiality_survive.trade_secrets: Trade secret information remains confidential perpetually or until it becomes public through no fault of Receiving Party

return_destruction.requirement: Within 30 days of termination or request, Receiving Party shall either: (A) Return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party, or (B) Permanently destroy it
destruction_process: Physical destruction: Shredding, incineration, or degaussing (for data storage)
Digital destruction: Secure deletion using NIST standards (Gutmann method or overwrite 7+ times)
Cloud data: Verified deletion from all servers and backup systems
Blockchain records: If applicable, archival with privacy-preserving hashing
return_destruction.certification: Receiving Party shall provide written certification of destruction signed by authorized officer

representations_warranties.rep_1: Each party represents it has full authority to enter into this Agreement and bind itself to its terms
representations_warranties.rep_2: Disclosing Party represents it has the right to disclose the Confidential Information and that disclosure does not violate any third-party rights
representations_warranties.rep_3: Receiving Party represents it will comply with all applicable laws including data protection, cybersecurity, and Islamic financing regulations
representations_warranties.rep_4: No confidentiality obligations exist in relation to Authorized Personnel as stated (they are duly bound)

limitation_liability.limitation_1: Confidential Information is provided 'AS IS' without warranty of accuracy, completeness, or fitness for any purpose
limitation_liability.limitation_2: Neither party assumes liability for decisions made based on Confidential Information
limitation_liability.limitation_3: Maximum liability capped at amount of direct damages actually incurred, not to exceed [SPECIFY: Amount in local currency]
limitation_liability.limitation_4: Neither party liable for indirect, consequential, punitive, or lost profits damages

remedy_1.title: Injunctive Relief
remedy_1.description: Disclosing Party acknowledges that monetary damages may be inadequate for breach of this Agreement. In event of breach or threatened breach, Disclosing Party may seek injunctive relief and specific performance without proving actual damages

remedy_2.title: Audit Rights
remedy_2.description: Disclosing Party may audit Receiving Party's compliance at any time with 5 business days' notice, no more than 2 times per year. Audits conducted at Disclosing Party's expense unless major breach discovered
remedy_2.audit_scope: Review of access logs, security measures, destruction records, and Authorized Personnel registers

remedy_3.title: Digital Forensics
remedy_3.description: In case of suspected breach, Disclosing Party may retain third-party digital forensics firm to investigate unauthorized access or disclosure. If breach confirmed, costs borne by Receiving Party

method_1.description: Parties shall attempt to resolve disputes through good-faith negotiation. If unresolved, proceed to Islamic mediation (Tahkim-based) with mutually agreed mediator

method_2.description: Disputes shall be resolved by binding arbitration under rules of [SPECIFY: Saudi Arbitration Law / DIAC / GCC Arbitration Center], conducted in Arabic
method_2.arbitrator_count: Single arbitrator for disputes < [Amount] or 3 arbitrators for larger disputes

method_3.order: If disputes involve Islamic financing or Sharia-compliant terms
method_3.description: Sharia-related disputes escalated to mutually agreed Islamic law scholar or Sharia council for fatwa/advisory opinion

method_4.order: Final recourse only if arbitration fails
method_4.venue: Courts of Saudi Arabia (Riyadh Regional Court preferred)

provision_1.title: Blockchain Verification (Optional)
provision_1.description: For high-value Confidential Information, parties may mutually agree to store hash of agreement and key access events on blockchain for immutable audit trail
provision_1.blockchain_chain: Choose: Ethereum, Hyperledger, or Saudi-approved blockchain
provision_1.benefit: Tamper-proof record of who accessed what and when

provision_2.title: AI Model Protection
provision_2.description: If Confidential Information includes AI models, source code, or training data: (A) Receiving Party shall not reverse-engineer, fine-tune on own data, or create derivative models without explicit written consent; (B) Receiving Party shall implement digital watermarking to identify model provenance
provision_2.enforcement: Breach of this provision subjects Receiving Party to greater statutory damages

provision_3.title: Islamic Financing Confidentiality
provision_3.description: If Confidential Information relates to Islamic financing products or Zakat calculations: (A) Receiving Party shall keep Islamic financial models and calculations confidential; (B) Shall not disclose to non-Islamic institutions; (C) Shall not use for Riba-based products
provision_3.oath: Receiving Party authorized personnel shall take formal oath (Bayah) to uphold Islamic principles

provision_4.title: Geofencing & Data Residency
provision_4.description: Confidential Information (especially customer data or IP) shall NEVER leave GCC region without Disclosing Party's written approval. Technical controls (IP geofencing, access controls) shall restrict access from outside approved locations
provision_4.monitoring: Real-time monitoring of data access and transfer locations with automated alerts if violations detected

nda_template_master.entire_agreement: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding the subject matter and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements, whether written or oral
nda_template_master.amendments: This Agreement may only be amended by written instrument signed by authorized representatives of both parties
nda_template_master.governing_law: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Saudi Arabia, consistent with Islamic Sharia principles
nda_template_master.severability: If any provision is found invalid, such provision shall be severed and remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect
nda_template_master.confidentiality_clause_itself: This Agreement itself shall be deemed Confidential Information and Receiving Party shall not disclose its existence or terms without prior written consent, except as required by law

automation_feature_1.description: Automatically embed digital watermarks in all Confidential Information files with metadata: creator, date, recipient, expiry
automation_feature_1.implementation: Python PIL library for images, PDF stamp for documents, Git metadata for code
automation_feature_1.monitoring: Automated system checks if watermarked files appear in unauthorized locations

automation_feature_2.description: Every access to Confidential Information logged with: who accessed, when, from where (IP/device), duration, what they accessed
automation_feature_2.implementation: Elasticsearch for indexing, Kibana for visualization, real-time alerts for suspicious access

automation_feature_3.description: On agreement expiration, automatically trigger secure deletion of all Confidential Information from receiving party's systems
automation_feature_3.implementation: Scheduled jobs, secure deletion protocol (7-pass overwrite), blockchain-certified proof of deletion
automation_feature_3.confirmation: Automated email confirmation to Disclosing Party with deletion certificate

automation_feature_4.description: ML model trained to detect suspicious access patterns: bulk downloads, unusual times, geographic anomalies, copy/paste activities
automation_feature_4.implementation: Isolation Forest algorithm, continuous learning from historical access patterns
automation_feature_4.alerts: Real-time alerts to both parties, escalation to legal team if high-risk activity detected

automation_feature_5.description: Automatically notify parties 90 days before NDA expiration with renewal options or destruction procedures
automation_feature_5.implementation: Scheduled notifications, automated renewal documents with pre-filled terms

automation_feature_6.description: Store cryptographic hashes of NDA, access logs, and key events on blockchain for immutable verification
automation_feature_6.implementation: Ethereum smart contracts with event logging, Hyperledger for private networks
automation_feature_6.benefit: Legally admissible evidence of compliance in court proceedings

policy_1.policy_statement: All information classified as Public, Internal, Confidential, or Restricted based on sensitivity and business impact

policy_2.policy_statement: Share only the minimum data necessary for the stated business purpose. Default to 'no share' unless explicit permission granted

policy_3.policy_statement: Data received under NDA may be used ONLY for the stated business purpose. Any other use requires new written consent

policy_4.policy_statement: Receiving Party shall NOT share or process Confidential Information through third-party sub-processors without explicit written consent of Disclosing Party

policy_5.policy_statement: All handling of Confidential Information must comply with Islamic principles: Amanah (Trust), Sirr (Secrecy), honest dealing, no Riba, no Gharar
